name: Crab Quiche Squares
recipeIngredient: 1 1 1/2 cups Shredded Gouda or Edam, Swiss cheese
recipeInstructions:
Equipment: Food processor, pastry blender or 2 knives, 9-inch (23 cm) square pan
Crust:
1. Preheat oven to 325°F (160°C).
2. Combine flour and salt in food processor or mixing bowl.
3. Add Crisco shortening. Cut in with pastry blender or two knives, or process in food processor until mixture resembles coarse meal.
4. Press mixture firmly into 9-inch (23 cm) square pan.
5. Bake at 325°F (160°C) for 10 minutes.
Filling:
6. Beat eggs, cream, dill weed, and salt together until blended.
7. Stir in cheese, crabmeat, and onion.
8. Pour over partially baked crust, spreading ingredients evenly.
9. Bake at 325°F (160°C) for 35 to 45 minutes, or until set in center.
10. Let stand 10 minutes, then cut into small squares and serve warm.
Makes: About 25 squares
Freezing: Excellent
Helpful Hints: Quiche squares can be prepared ahead and refrigerated 1 day or frozen 3 months. Prepare and bake, cool and cut into squares. Place in flat container and wrap air-tight. To reheat, thaw if frozen and heat, uncovered at 350°F (180°C) for about 15 minutes or until hot.
